Create Liquidity Floor Cutoffs

Source: R/constructors.R
create_liquidity_floor_cutoffs.Rd

Construct a liquidity_floor_cutoffs data frame from scratch.

Usage

create_liquidity_floor_cutoffs(metric_name, metric_cutoffs)

Arguments

metric_name

A character vector of metric names.

metric_cutoffs

A list of named numeric vectors, one for each metric in metric_name. Each vector must have names exactly equal to c("micro_caps", "small_caps", "mid_caps", "large_caps", "mega_caps") (order may vary).

Value

A data.frame with a column liquidity_classification and one column per metric. The rows are ordered (after reordering each metric vector according to the allowed levels) so that the main liquidity metric (assumed to be the first element of metric_name) is in non-decreasing order.

Details

The function enforces that:

  • The returned object is a data.frame.

  • The column names (besides liquidity_classification) are numeric with no NAs.

  • The liquidity_classification values are exactly the allowed levels: "micro_caps", "small_caps", "mid_caps", "large_caps", "mega_caps".

  • The main liquidity metric (the first metric in metric_name) is in ascending order.

  • The ordering (ranking) of liquidity classifications is consistent across metrics.

On this page
